user.title: Set up Lightener
user.description: Lightener uses a visual editor to set up groups — pick lights, shape brightness curves on a live graph, and try presets without typing numbers. Click Submit to continue in the **Lightener Editor**.

area.title: Filter by area (optional)
area.description: Select a room or area to narrow down the lights on the next screen. With 100+ lights this saves a lot of scrolling. Leave blank to see all lights.

lights.title: Select lights
lights.description: Pick the lights to control. You'll tune each light's brightness curve visually in the **Lightener Editor** in the next step — no number-crunching here.

data_description.brightness: One mapping per line as group:light. Example: "60:100" means at 60% group brightness, "{light_name}" reaches 100%.

error.invalid_brightness: Invalid entry "{error_entry}". Use the format "X:Y" where X is 1–100 and Y is 0–100, e.g. "40:100"

create_entry.open_editor: Group created. **[Tune the curves now →]({editor_url})** — the editor is where you pick presets and shape each light's response on the live graph.

abort.open_editor: **[Continue in Lightener Editor →]({editor_url})** — pick lights, choose a curve preset, and shape the response on the live graph.

init.title: Select lights
init.description: Add or remove lights here. Open **Lightener Editor** in the sidebar to pick presets and tune curves visually — new lights start with a linear curve.

data_description.brightness: One mapping per line as group:light. Example: "60:100" means at 60% group brightness, "{light_name}" reaches 100%.

error.invalid_brightness: Invalid entry "{error_entry}". Use the format "X:Y" where X is 1–100 and Y is 0–100, e.g. "40:100"
